idea.title: Shader Polish Sprint
idea.hook: Drag screen-space VFX layers (bloom, chromatic, grain, scanline, vignette, dither) onto a flat ugly prototype until a fickle FEEL meter clicks into the green zone — but each layer costs polish-budget and over-stacking tanks the score.
idea.hook_ru: Перетаскивай слои пост-эффектов на тусклый прототип, пока FEEL не попадёт в зелёную зону — каждый слой стоит бюджета, а перебор обнуляет счёт.
idea.prototype: Left half of canvas: live-running tiny dummy game (square hopping over pegs). Right half: 6 VFX layer tiles (bloom 1, chromatic 2, grain 3, scanline 1, vignette 2, dither 3 polish points). Each layer has a feel delta that shifts a hidden target curve. Player starts with 10 polish points. Goal: get FEEL meter into target zone (random per level, daily-seeded) using fewest points. Stacking 5+ layers triggers OVERPRODUCED penalty that doubles distance-to-target. 8 levels per run. Lose = polish points hit 0 before all 8 locks. Mouse drag controls.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Polish budget (10 points) + OVERPRODUCED penalty (5+ stacked layers = distance doubles). No countdown timer. Score-target + resource-scarcity mechanics.
idea.why_novel: First arcade where the controls are post-processing knobs and the win-state is a designer's gut feeling — the dummy game itself never changes mechanics, but stacking the wrong VFX makes the same scene 'feel' wrong. Meta-game about the game-design polish phase nobody else has gamified.

sources.title: Shader Polish Sprint
sources.snippet: Idea #3: drag VFX layers onto a tiny prototype until the FEEL meter clicks green.

sources.title: Post-process polish phase
sources.snippet: The polish phase is where indie devs stack shaders until the game 'feels right' — a tweetable craft skill nobody has gamified.

sources.title: Feel-curve calibration
sources.snippet: Game feel comes from layered tiny post-effects you can't articulate but you know when they snap in.

synthesizer_brief: Drag-and-drop puzzler where VFX layers are the verbs and a hidden target FEEL curve is the win-state. Tiny self-running dummy game on the left, 6 layer tiles on the right, budget bar on the HUD. Stacking too many triggers OVERPRODUCED. 8 daily-seeded target zones per run. Mouse + touch drag, single LOCK button per level.

alternatives.title: Token Triage
alternatives.hook: Drag AI requests onto cheap/expensive model lanes before the budget empties
alternatives.why_novel: Conveyor sort + tiered cost economy on agent-cost news

alternatives.title: Difficulty Curve
alternatives.hook: Drag tower-defense waves up or down to keep the fun-meter inside a moving target band

alternatives.title: Tier-1 Hook
alternatives.hook: Assemble a global-market store page from cards and beat the AI buyer panel
alternatives.why_novel: Reveal-on-commit card drafting with regional buyer reactions

alternatives.title: Editor Agent
alternatives.hook: Place AI agents on a scene grid to auto-fix bugs before queue overflows
